tools.name: ask_demand_discovery

tools.name: get_validation_framework
tools.description: Returns the full three-step Demand Discovery validation framework: (1) Market Research, (2) Demand Discovery Report with the Demand Score and Build/Pivot/Kill verdict, (3) Agentic Launch (90-day continuous outreach). Use when a user asks "how do I validate an idea?", "what's the methodology?", or wa

tools.name: get_product_details
tools.description: Returns the full product breakdown (Market Research, Demand Discovery Report, Agentic Launch) and pricing tiers (Starter $49, Founder Pack of 5 ideas, Studio Pack of 25 ideas, all using a slot-based model where pivoted/archived ideas free a slot for a new one). tools.name: explain_demand_signals
tools.description: Returns the four classes of real-world signal the Demand Discovery Report triangulates - search intent, outreach responses, landing-page engagement, and buying signals - and the three possible verdicts (Build, Pivot, Kill). Use when a user asks how the score works at a high level, why behavioral sig

tools.name: compare_validation_approaches
tools.description: Returns an honest comparison of how different validation approaches work - generic AI assistants, trend aggregators, passive scoring tools, and Demand Discovery AI - and where each one stops. tools.name: get_data_source_categories
tools.description: Returns the four behavioral data-source buckets - Search & attention, Conversation & pain, Adoption & spend, Capital & hiring - with each bucket's tagline and what it captures. Use when a user asks "what data sources do you use?", "where does the Demand Score come from?", or wants to understand how

tools.description: VERBATIM OUTPUT — READ FIRST (the most important rule for this tool): every piece of report text this tool returns (the Market Research summary AND the next-step message) is authored on our side. tools.name: validate_real_demand
tools.description: Check the account status for this idea's full Demand Discovery Report and return the user's next step. tools.name: get_demand_report_status
tools.description: Poll the status of a paid Demand Discovery Report after the user has started checkout with validate_real_demand. tools.name: start_agentic_launch
tools.description: Kick off Day 1 of the 90-day Agentic Launch for a completed Demand Discovery Report. Demand Discovery surfaces named prospects matching the idea's ICP and DRAFTS the first outreach batch. It sends NOTHING automatically - the user reviews and sends from their hosted manage page. Outreach is drafted t

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "start_demand_report" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: VERBATIM OUTPUT — READ FIRST (the most important rule for this tool): every piece of report text this tool returns (the Market Research summary AND the next-ste…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "start_demand_report" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: VERBATIM OUTPUT — READ FIRST (the most important rule for this tool): every piece of report text this tool returns (the Market Research summary AND the next-ste…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "start_demand_report" stacks urgency/override words (important, always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: VERBATIM OUTPUT — READ FIRST (the most important rule for this tool): every piece of report text this tool returns (the Market Research summary AND the next-ste…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: start_demand_report, validate_real_demand, get_demand_report_status · Private data: validate_real_demand · Public sink: start_demand_report, validate_real_demand, start_agentic_launch · Destructive: get_validation_framework, explain_demand_signals, start_demand_report, validate_real_demand, get_demand_report_status.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: start_demand_report, validate_real_demand, get_demand_report_status · Private data: validate_real_demand · Public sink: start_demand_report, validate_real_demand, start_agentic_launch · Destructive: get_validation_framework, explain_demand_signals, start_demand_report, validate_real_demand, get_demand_report_status

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
